title: Disobedience to Parents
subtitle: Ingratitude to Parents — Paired with Ingratitude to Allah

verses.arabic: وَقَضَىٰ رَبُّكَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا ۚ إِمَّا يَبْلُغَنَّ عِندَكَ الْكِبَرَ أَحَدُهُمَا أَوْ كِلَاهُمَا فَلَا تَقُل لَّهُمَا أُفٍّ وَلَا تَنْهَرْهُمَا وَقُل لَّهُمَا قَوْلًا كَرِيمًا
verses.translation: And your Lord has decreed that you not worship except Him, and to parents, good treatment. Whether one or both of them reach old age [while] with you, say not to them [so much as] 'uff,' and do not repel them but speak to them a noble word.

verses.arabic: وَاخْفِضْ لَهُمَا جَنَاحَ الذُّلِّ مِنَ الرَّحْمَةِ وَقُل رَّبِّ ارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
verses.translation: And lower to them the wing of humility out of mercy and say, 'My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up [when I was] small.'

verses.arabic: وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنسَانَ بِوَالِدَيْهِ إِحْسَانًا ۖ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ كُرْهًا وَوَضَعَتْهُ كُرْهًا
verses.translation: And We have enjoined upon man, to his parents, good treatment. His mother carried him with hardship and gave birth to him with hardship.

verses.arabic: وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنسَانَ بِوَالِدَيْهِ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ وَهْنًا عَلَىٰ وَهْنٍ وَفِصَالُهُ فِي عَامَيْنِ أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيْكَ إِلَيَّ الْمَصِيرُ
verses.translation: And We have enjoined upon man [care] for his parents. His mother carried him, [increasing her] in weakness upon weakness, and his weaning is in two years. Be grateful to Me and to your parents; to Me is the [final] destination.

brief_explanation: The Quran places kindness to parents directly alongside the worship of Allah alone — the only two commandments linked in such a direct and inseparable pairing throughout the Quran. Even the slightest verbal slight ('uff') is explicitly forbidden, making disobedience to parents one of the most sensitively treated sins in Islamic ethics. Allah describes the sacrifice of mothers in carrying and bearing children with hardship, establishing a debt of gratitude that no child can fully repay. The Prophet (peace be upon him) named 'uquq al-walidayn (disobedience to parents) among the three greatest sins alongside shirk and false testimony, and declared it a sin that earns punishment both in this world and in the Hereafter.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd execution for disobedience to parents. It is one of the kabair (major sins) punished through divine reckoning on the Day of Judgment. The Islamic state may impose ta'zir (discretionary punishment) for extreme cases. Scholars note, however, that if a child harms or kills a parent, the normal rulings of assault and murder apply — though the Hanafi school holds that a father cannot be executed for killing his child (based on hadith), the reverse is not the case.
